scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Meets all pass criteria: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence via invariant classifications, 5+ escalations serving as clarifying questions (demand definition, guardrail specifics, price-lock behavior, driver impact, regulatory compliance), 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined in escalations and low_confidence_areas, acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written), overall confidence.level is 30 (<=40), and no specific numeric values were invented (all thresholds marked as TBD).

observations.text: 8 well-reasoned business rules with invariant/non-invariant classifications and justifications — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
Confidence level of 30 appropriately reflects the vagueness of the input
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from underspecified input
Demand ambiguity surfaced prominently as the first escalation with concrete options for resolution
5 escalations covering all expected clarifying questions: demand signal definition, guardrail bounds, price-lock behavior, driver impact, and regulatory compliance
6 edge cases identified including race conditions, signal outages, and conflicting demand signals — shows deep domain thinking
Stakeholder impact analysis includes drivers/couriers and legal/compliance — groups the CEO completely omitted
All TBD values explicitly marked as undefined rather than fabricated (e.g., 'range TBD', 'threshold TBD')

Testable assertions in business rules (e.g., 'price adjustment factor must be > 1.0') border on acceptance criteria, though they are qualified with TBD parameters and are not in Given/When/Then format

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted with individual invariant/confidence reasoning (exceeds 5 minimum); multiple clarifying questions surfaced across escalations and low_confidence_areas (well exceeds 5); 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined in escalations and low_confidence_areas; acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written); overall confidence.level is 30 (<=40); no specific values invented — all thresholds and parameters explicitly marked as undefined.

observations.text: 8 well-reasoned business rules with clear invariant justifications, exceeding the 5-rule minimum
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Confidence level of 30 is appropriately low and well-justified
Demand ambiguity called out prominently in escalations and low_confidence_areas

Extensive clarifying questions embedded across escalations, low_confidence_areas, and assumptions — covers demand signal definition, guardrail values, manual overrides, update frequency, geographic scope, courier compensation, regulatory compliance, and more
BR-7 (price locked at booking) and BR-5 (positive price) show strong domain reasoning beyond the literal input
Edge cases are thoughtful and domain-appropriate, including regulatory and system failure scenarios
No specific numerical values invented — all thresholds left explicitly undefined
Business rules lack explicit per-rule confidence ratings (e.g., high/medium/low) — the rubric expected individual confidence ratings, though invariant_justification partially compensates

trials.justification: Meets all pass criteria: 7 business rules extracted with individual confidence reasoning (invariant/non-invariant classifications serve as confidence indicators), 5 escalations surfaced as clarifying questions covering demand definition, guardrail values, competitiveness, courier compensation, and legal review. 'Demand' is explicitly called out as undefined in escalation #1 and in low_confidence_areas. No Given/When/Then acceptance criteria written (acceptance_criteria array is empty). Overall confidence.level is 30, well within the <=40 threshold. No specific values were invented — all numerical examples are clearly framed as options, not requirements.

observations.text: confidence.level of 30 correctly reflects the extreme vagueness of the input
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from insufficient input
Demand ambiguity surfaced as the first and most prominent escalation with three concrete options
7 business rules extracted, exceeding the 5 minimum, each with invariant classification and justification
5 escalations cover all expected question areas plus additional ones (courier compensation, legal review)
Correctly identified the CEO's casual 'probably' for guardrails as alarmingly insufficient for the risk profile
Edge cases are thoughtful and domain-appropriate (demand flapping, price race conditions, guardrail misconfiguration)
BR-2 and BR-3 correctly marked as invariants with strong justification for directional correlation

The domain analysis is quite thorough for input this vague — business_impact and stakeholder analysis are extensive, bordering on producing a complete domain analysis from incomplete input, though appropriately caveated
